task_completion.visual_evidence: Jenkins web interface showing the project/job page specifically for 'Nightly-Backup'.

task_completion.description: Open the configuration settings for the job
task_completion.visual_evidence: Jenkins configuration page is open, indicated by 'Configure' in the UI and settings sections like General, Source Code Management, or Build Triggers.
In the 'Build Triggers' section of the configuration page, the checkbox next to 'Build periodically' is checked.

task_completion.description: Enter the correct cron syntax for midnight execution
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Schedule text box under 'Build periodically' contains exactly the text 'H 0 * * *'.
The agent clicks the 'Save' button at the bottom of the configuration page, or the screen transitions back to the 'Nightly-Backup' project dashboard after the settings have been applied.

integrity.description: Agent used a web browser to configure Jenkins
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show a web browser interface (like Chrome or Firefox) navigating the Jenkins UI, rather than terminal windows executing API commands.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the genuine Jenkins application
integrity.visual_evidence: The UI elements, styling, and layout match the standard Jenkins web interface, not a text editor or a mock HTML page.

integrity.description: Agent did not use terminal commands to bypass the GUI
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows are shown using commands like 'curl', 'wget', or editing 'config.xml' directly to apply the schedule.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected Jenkins job name is 'Nightly-Backup'. The expected cron schedule syntax is 'H 0 * * *', which is valid Jenkins syntax for running daily at midnight with hash-based load distribution.
